b2-vix-reversion.name: b2-vix-reversion
b2-vix-reversion.mechanism: Volatility spikes overshoot because protection is bought at any price; when VIX closes far above its recent norm vol sellers re-enter and equities rebound next session

zc-rma-volatility-factor-five-day-window.name: zc-rma-volatility-factor-five-day-window
zc-rma-volatility-factor-five-day-window.mechanism: USDA RMA sets the corn crop-insurance volatility factor from implied vols averaged over only the final five trading days of February, fixing premium economics for roughly $100bn of revenue-protection coverage; insurers, reinsurers, and approved providers therefore have vol exposure marked in a precisely dated five-session slot and transact (vol-hedge / delta-hedge) around it, which should show up as elevated realized-range / breakout behavior in ZC futures on those specific five sessions, distinct from the whole-February MTD price-average mean-reversion mechanism already tested (zc-crop-insurance-feb-discovery, hash babd2e0218808469).

zc-farm-cash-rent-duedate-liquidation-wi.name: zc-farm-cash-rent-duedate-liquidation-window
zc-farm-cash-rent-duedate-liquidation-wi.mechanism: US cash farm rent is contractually due on fixed calendar dates (primary instalment March 1, second instalment November 1, per Midwest cash-rent literature e.g. Iowa State Extension / USDA ERS EIB-219) and farmers holding stored grain must raise cash by those dates regardless of price, producing concentrated forced commercial selling in the sessions leading into the deadline; the buyer of that inventory is compensated with a price discount, so ZC should drift down into each due date.

mes-crude-liquidity-channel-risk-transfe.name: mes-crude-liquidity-channel-risk-transfer
mes-crude-liquidity-channel-risk-transfe.mechanism: Crude oil is the dominant SOURCE of intraday liquidity spillover across futures, and that liquidity channel is separate from the return channel: when WTI's price-impact coefficient (Amihud illiquidity) spikes, dealers' balance-sheet capacity for commodity-flow risk-bearing is constrained, and that same constraint reprices equity index risk with a one-session lag because the same dealer balance sheets intermediate both books; whoever supplies equity-index liquidity the NEXT session is paid a liquidity-provision premium, which shows up as a next-session down-drift in MES that is a supply/liquidity effect, not a directional crude-return effect (MCL's own return tails give only -6bp/-4bp vs MES -16.5bp/base +2.2bp on the illiquidity-conditioned cut). Per Miao & Yang (JFM 2026), commonality in intraday futures liquidity is supply-side driven and liquidity spillovers do not mirror return spillovers -- this candidate trades that spillover directionally: SHORT MES the session after MCL prints unusually high illiquidity.

zs-china-state-reserve-auction-window.name: zs-china-state-reserve-auction-window
zs-china-state-reserve-auction-window.mechanism: Sinograin/state-reserve rotation auctions for soybeans and oilseeds are scheduled events that either withdraw supply from or release supply into the domestic Chinese market on a known day; board crush and export-basis participants must adjust hedges into that announced date, so risk transfers on a schedule rather than on news, producing hedge-flow pressure in the sessions leading into the recurring auction date.

scan-mbt.name: mbt-nr7 (bitcoin)
scan-mbt.mechanism: MARKET-EXPANSION SCAN of MBT (micro bitcoin): the five validated mechanism configs (monday, nr7, prehol, vix-rebound, open-drive) run natively; each market is judged on its own merits per Daniel's scope-opening order — no cross-market consistency required, and every config counted as a trial

b3-nr7-breakout.name: b3-nr7-breakout
b3-nr7-breakout.mechanism: Volatility compression precedes expansion; a break out of the narrowest range in 7 days carries real information

b10-nagel-vix-reversal.name: b10-nagel-vix-reversal
b10-nagel-vix-reversal.mechanism: Short-horizon reversal is the return to liquidity provision and spikes when intermediaries retreat (high VIX) — you are paid to absorb urgency when it is scarcest (Nagel RFS 2012)

b3-monday-long.name: b3-monday-long
b3-monday-long.mechanism: Weekend risk premium and Monday retail inflows favor longs at the week open; the shelf's Monday sleeve has never been honestly measured on real data

mes-gold-overnight-macro-signal-open-con.name: mes-gold-overnight-macro-signal-open-continuation
mes-gold-overnight-macro-signal-open-con.mechanism: Gold trades a deep London session while US equity cash is shut, so an overnight real-rate/risk repricing gets fully expressed in MGC but only partially in thin equity futures; the US equity open is the first moment that repricing meets real depth and index desks complete it, producing sign-matched MES continuation after the open.

mnq-event-free-day-flow-persistence.name: mnq-event-free-day-flow-persistence
mnq-event-free-day-flow-persistence.mechanism: On days with no scheduled macro release (no FOMC/CPI on the calendar), intraday price movement is driven by inventory and order-flow imbalance rather than information, so the first-60-minute directional displacement reflects a liquidity-provider inventory imbalance that must keep being paid for through the rest of the session; on scheduled-event days an information shock resets order books and kills that persistence, so the edge should only appear when the macro calendar is empty.

b3-preholiday-drift.name: b3-preholiday-drift
b3-preholiday-drift.mechanism: Documented pre-holiday drift: shorts cover and liquidity providers mark up ahead of exchange closures

b4-open-drive.name: b4-open-drive
b4-open-drive.mechanism: An open-drive (three one-directional opening bars closing at the extreme) is initiative institutional flow that does not revert intraday

mes-settle-tradable-divergence.name: mes-settle-tradable-divergence
mes-settle-tradable-divergence.mechanism: Variation margin and fund NAVs are struck off the RTH-close (settle proxy) while a flat-overnight participant only captures the open-to-open return; on days where settle-to-settle and open-to-open returns disagree in sign, the forced position adjustment implied by the settle-based return lands in the following RTH session, so taking that side captures the unwind.

mcl-inside-week-unresolved-positioning-c.name: mcl-inside-week-unresolved-positioning-carryover
mcl-inside-week-unresolved-positioning-c.mechanism: A week that trades entirely inside the prior week's RTH high/low fires no weekly-horizon trigger for the systematic cohort (weekly-refresh trend models, weekly option writers), so positioning and inventory carry forward unchanged instead of being reset by de-risking flow, letting the following week's move run unimpeded on MCL.

zn-fr2004-dealer-inventory-capacity-stat.name: zn-fr2004-dealer-inventory-capacity-state
zn-fr2004-dealer-inventory-capacity-stat.mechanism: The NY Fed publishes primary-dealer Treasury positions weekly (FR2004). When reported dealer net long duration sits high in its own trailing distribution, intermediation balance sheet capacity is already committed, so the next increment of one-sided customer flow gets absorbed at a worse price and reverts less afterward -- a momentum-continuation effect conditioned on a published capacity STATE, distinct from calendar-event auction-concession trades that decay. PROXY (no FR2004 panel exists in this lab): weekly signed RTH volume flow on ZN itself stands in for the capacity-state variable, since no dealer position feed is available; this is a stated, honest limitation, not the real FR2004 series.

mcl-quarter-end-funding-stress-forced-un.name: mcl-quarter-end-funding-stress-forced-unwind-vs-normal-quarter-end
mcl-quarter-end-funding-stress-forced-un.mechanism: Quarter-end balance-sheet withdrawal by dealers is predictable in timing but not in severity: forced unwinds by leveraged holders only occur when repo terms tighten and futures margin rise together, so this candidate is paid by leveraged holders liquidating into a shrinking intermediation capacity, gated by the observed funding-stress intensity of THIS quarter-end (proxied by the prior-session VIX close versus its trailing 20-session median) relative to the average quarter-end in the window, not by the calendar date alone.

mes-wk10-derisk.name: mes-wk10-derisk
mes-wk10-derisk.mechanism: Week 10 of the calendar quarter, the week before quarterly index option/futures expiry, is when dealers and levered funds cut risk ahead of the largest delta-reset of the quarter, producing a synchronized cross-asset de-risk before re-establishing after expiry.

mgc-china-lpr-fix-evening-window.name: mgc-china-lpr-fix-evening-window
mgc-china-lpr-fix-evening-window.mechanism: China Loan Prime Rate is fixed at 09:15 Beijing time on the 20th of each month; the print lands ~20:00-23:00 ET the evening before (the 19th), a thin Globex window with only Asian desks marking gold, so SGE-side repricing of rate-sensitive physical/retail demand transmits into COMEX gold before US desks arrive to arbitrage it away.

mes-petrodollar-recycling-sovereign-infl.name: mes-petrodollar-recycling-sovereign-inflow-lag
mes-petrodollar-recycling-sovereign-infl.mechanism: Sustained high crude oil revenue accrues to oil-exporting sovereign wealth funds, which redeploy into global equities on a budgeting cycle that lags the revenue accrual by roughly one quarter, so a slow flow-driven equity bid is conditional on LAGGED cumulative crude return rather than contemporaneous crude moves.

decisions.title: Eleventh graduate, with a caveat: September RTH weakness (PF 1.51 / 1.405)
decisions.recommendation: HOLD — passed every gate, but the evidence is weaker than the numbers look (engine's honest read)
decisions.evidence: dev3yr: n=61, PF 1.51, $29.29/trade shorting MES day-sessions in September | robustness: SURVIVES | replication: n=61, PF 1.405 on MNQ
decisions.what_yes_means: Book grows by one; sizing and all bundles recompute including it
decisions.recap: US equity indexes have historically been weak in September during regular trading hours. This shorts that window. It passed all three gates.
decisions.why: Why the gates passed but the engine still hesitates: (1) a September seasonal in a 3-year window samples only THREE Septembers — n=61 trades but really 3 independent yearly events; (2) the replication ran on MNQ, which moves almost identically to MES day-to-day, so for a calendar effect the sibling test is nearly the same bet twice, not independent confirmation; (3) September weakness is a widely-published anomaly, which cuts both ways — real historical basis, but also heavily arbitraged. If you admit it, its bundle weight will be small either way. Waiting costs nothing: it stays a preserved candidate and the next real September is out-of-sample evidence for free.
steps: Approve: joins the book despite the engine's hesitation (your call, like the RMA window)
Deny: remains a replicated candidate, fully preserved
Question: the card returns with any analysis you want
Live/paper exposure still requires its own GO regardless

decisions.title: Tenth machine-discovered sleeve ready: petrodollar recycling lag (PF 1.687, n=93)
decisions.recommendation: ADMIT to the book — strong dual-leg (dev PF 1.687, replication PF 1.544 on MNQ, n=93 both legs)
decisions.evidence: dev3yr: n=93, PF 1.687, $29.42/trade on MES | robustness: SURVIVES | replication: n=93, PF 1.544 on MNQ — both legs strong on real samples
decisions.what_yes_means: Book grows by one; sizing and all bundles recompute including it
decisions.recap: After large oil-revenue windfalls, sovereign wealth flows reach US equities with a measurable lag. The strategy trades that delayed inflow on the S&P, and the same lag shows up independently on the Nasdaq.
decisions.why: It cleared every gate with margin: dev PF 1.687 and replication PF 1.544 are both far above their bars on 93-trade samples — no thin-sample caveat, no near-miss margins. The mechanism (petrodollar recycling) is a documented macro flow, structurally distinct from every sleeve in the book.
steps: Approve: strategy joins the validated book; sizing/bundles/roadmap recompute (golden card auto-updates if the book improves)
Deny: remains a replicated candidate, fully preserved
Question: the card returns with any analysis you want
Live/paper exposure still requires its own GO regardless

decisions.title: GOLDEN TEST: Validated book (all approved sleeves) projects $13,230/mo
decisions.recommendation: APPROVE — clears the $10k/mo goal on verified rules, fully deployable
decisions.evidence: 18 x Apex, breach 18%, frozen at golden_1784823936.json (supersedes the earlier 12-sleeve freeze after your RMA approval)
decisions.what_yes_means: Spends one irreversible holdout look on data after 2025-03-01
decisions.recap: Runs the frozen bundle once on the untouched holdout data. Pass means it is real and paper trading becomes the next conversation.
decisions.why: Every number so far comes from the dev window. The holdout is the only data that can't have been overfit — one look, spent only when the projection already clears the goal.
steps: Frozen bundle config is loaded (already immutable on disk)
Each sleeve runs once on holdout data (>= 2025-03-01), costs included
Results recorded to the ledger as a holdout look (the sacred counter increments)
PASS -> bundle flagged paper-ready on the dashboard; FAIL -> back to the funnel, holdout look spent
Nothing trades real or paper money either way without your further GO

decisions.title: Fifth machine-discovered sleeve ready: quarterly week-10 pre-expiry derisk (PF 2.106)
decisions.recommendation: ADMIT to the book — strongest dual-leg showing yet (dev PF 2.106, replication PF 1.607)
decisions.evidence: dev3yr: n=52, PF 2.106, $48.93/trade | robustness: SURVIVES | replication: PF 1.607 on MNQ vs 1.10 bar — both legs emphatic, and the fat-$/trade profile matches your preferred family
decisions.what_yes_means: Book grows by one; sizing and all bundles recompute including it
decisions.recap: In week 10 of each quarterly expiry cycle, institutions de-risk ahead of expiry and the strategy rides that flow on MES. Passed every gate today with the n>=15 rule enforced.
decisions.why: Rare-firing, high expectancy, works on both index siblings — the exact risk-premium-harvesting profile of your pinned vix-reversion. One consideration: it is calendar-cycle-flavored; the book currently has no quarterly-cycle sleeve so direct overlap looks low, but if you want a formal overlap report vs the monday/TOM/prehol family before deciding, tap Question and the engine will run it.
steps: Approve: strategy joins the validated book; sizing/bundles/roadmap recompute
Question: ask for the calendar-overlap report (recommended if unsure) — card returns with the analysis
Deny: remains a replicated candidate, fully preserved
Live/paper exposure still requires its own GO regardless

decisions.title: Fourth machine-discovered sleeve ready: depth-consumption range forecast (PF 1.168)
decisions.recommendation: ADMIT to the book — full funnel passed with a strong replication (PF 1.313 on MNQ)
decisions.evidence: dev3yr: n=124, PF 1.168, $9.1/trade | robustness: SURVIVES | replication: PF 1.313 on MNQ vs 1.10 bar — a solid carry, not a graze
decisions.what_yes_means: Book grows by one; sizing and all bundles recompute including it
decisions.recap: When early-session prints consume unusually deep liquidity, the session's range expands predictably and the strategy positions for it. Discovered, hardened, and replicated by the engine this afternoon.
decisions.why: Cleared every pre-registered gate with a replication margin (1.313 vs 1.10) comparable to gold-signal's — the strong-carry profile that distinguishes the two admitted graduates from the borderline ones. Microstructure mechanism with liquidity-consumption logic rather than calendar luck.

decisions.title: Third machine-discovered sleeve ready: crude closure-length reopen overshoot (PF 1.325)
decisions.recommendation: ADMIT to the book — full funnel passed with a solid replication (PF 1.143 on MNG, n~67)
decisions.evidence: dev3yr: n=154, PF 1.325, $14.28/trade | robustness: SURVIVES (incl. a dose-response diagnostic: longer closures -> bigger overshoots) | replication: PF 1.143 on MNG vs 1.10 bar, sibling window honestly truncated to MNG data start (2023-11)
decisions.what_yes_means: Book grows by one; sizing and all bundles recompute including it
decisions.recap: After a trading halt or weekend closure on crude, the reopen overshoots in proportion to how long the market was closed, then retraces. Discovered, hardened, and replicated by the engine this morning.
decisions.why: It cleared every pre-registered gate, and unlike most reopen ideas it shows a dose-response: longer closures produce bigger overshoots, which is what a real pent-up-flow mechanism predicts. Honest caveat: the MNG replication window is shorter than ideal (sibling data begins 2023-11), declared before the run rather than discovered after.
steps: Approve: strategy joins the validated book; sizing/bundles/roadmap recompute
Deny: remains a replicated candidate, fully preserved
